summary: American Ashcan School painter, student of Robert Henri, known for boxing scenes (Stag at Sharkey's, Both Members of This Club, Dempsey and Firpo), urban New York street scenes (Cliff Dwellers, 42 Kids), and WWI atrocity paintings (The Barricade). Editor/illustrator for the socialist magazine The Masses.

works.name: Boxing Paintings (Stag at Sharkey's, Both Members of This Club, Dempsey and Firpo)
works.description: Series of boxing paintings, his most famous subject matter. Stag at Sharkey's (1909) depicts a match at the title club; Both Members of This Club satirizes racial exclusion; Dempsey and Firpo shows Luis Angel Firpo knocking Jack Dempsey over the ropes.

clues.clue: Stag at Sharkey's: boxing match at the title club, blue-shirted referee behind two men; set across the street from Bellows's studio; a man smoking a cigar glares back at the viewer
examples: two bloodied men grapple each other at a namesake boxing club
a boxing match attended by patrons of the title club
A man with a cigar cynically glares back at the viewer in that painting where several red-faced men watch this activity

clues.clue: Both Members of This Club: title refers to a trick to skirt the Lewis Law of 1900; satirizes exclusion of 'socially unacceptable' members; depicts black boxer (Joe Gans / possibly Jack Johnson)
examples: this man depicted the black Joe Gans in a painting satirizing the practice of barring 'socially unacceptable' members from joining private groups
This artist of Cliff Dwellers appended an ironic title to a painting showing a black fighter attacking a man with a bloodied face
A trick to skirt the Lewis Law of 1900 explains the title of one painting of this activity

clues.clue: Dempsey and Firpo: Luis Angel Firpo knocks Jack Dempsey over a rope/through rope
examples: Luis Angel Firpo knocks Jack Dempsey over a rope in a painting by this Ashcan artist
a surprised crowd as a man in shorts falls through rope

clues.clue: Bellows known for painting many boxing scenes as a subject matter
examples: this artist of urban New York noted for his many paintings of boxing scenes
this other American realist painter was a student of Henri and did paintings of boxers

works.name: Cliff Dwellers
works.description: Crowded urban street scene set in tenement New York, with clotheslines hanging between buildings.

clues.clue: Crowded tenement scene with clotheslines hanging between buildings; trolley heading toward Vesey Street; street vendor pushes yellow cart; used a color system based on musical notes learned from H.G. Maratta
examples: a crowded street scene in which a streetcar passes under clotheslines
depicted a vendor hawking goods in a crowded scene set underneath clotheslines hanging between tenement buildings
a trolley heads toward Vesey Street while a street vendor hawks goods from a pushcart

clues.clue: Woman breastfeeding baby in the hot summer scene
examples: a woman breastfeeds her baby below several clotheslines on a hot summer day

works.name: 42 Kids
works.description: Painting of nude boys/skinny dippers playing on a wooden pier on the East River, also known as 'Splinter Beach.'

clues.clue: Title number of skinny dippers / nude children playing on a wooden pier (East River 'Splinter Beach')
examples: this artist of The Barricade often depicted 'splinter beaches' as editor of The Masses, where skinny dippers play in his work 42 Kids
The socialist magazine The Masses exhibited this artist's drawings of 'Splinter Beach,' a wooden pier on the East River on which many nude children play

works.name: WWI Atrocity Paintings (The Barricade, The Germans Arrive, Massacre of Dinant)
works.description: Series of WWI paintings depicting German atrocities in Belgium. Criticized by Joseph Pennell for painting war scenes he hadn't witnessed.

clues.clue: The Barricade: German soldiers hiding behind naked Belgian civilians as a human barricade/shield
examples: German soldiers hiding behind naked bodies in The Barricade
nude Belgians holding up their arms in defiance against the German invasion

clues.clue: Bellows's retort that Leonardo 'had no ticket to paint the Last Supper' when criticized for painting scenes he didn't witness
examples: This artist asked if Da Vinci 'had a ticket to paint the Last Supper'
he was not aware that Leonardo 'had a ticket of admission to the Last Supper'

clues.clue: Joseph Pennell / critics claimed he had no right to paint WWI images
examples: Joseph Pennell claimed that this man had no right to paint images of WWI

clues.clue: The Germans Arrive: officer holds a boy whose hands have been severed; men in Prussian helmets fixing bayonets
examples: Men in Prussian helmets fix bayonets as an officer holds a boy whose hands have been severed

comprehensive_summary:
George Bellows (1882–1925) was an American painter and a leading figure in the Ashcan School, a movement of largely socialist realists who sought to capture gritty, realistic scenes of daily urban life in New York. He was a student of Robert Henri and also served as an illustrator and editor for the socialist magazine The Masses, where Art Young was the first critic to apply the term 'Ashcan' to painting. The Ashcan School had its origins in Philadelphia, where members studied under Thomas Anshutz.

Bellows is most famous for his boxing paintings: Stag at Sharkey's (1909) depicts a match attended by patrons of the title club, set across the street from Bellows's studio, featuring a blue-shirted referee behind two bloodied fighters and a man with a cigar cynically glaring back at the viewer. 'Stag' refers to non-members brought into the club to fight. Both Members of This Club satirizes racial exclusion — its title references a trick to skirt the Lewis Law of 1900, and depicts a black boxer (possibly inspired by Jack Johnson or Joe Gans). Dempsey and Firpo shows the dramatic moment when Luis Angel Firpo knocks Jack Dempsey over the ropes into the crowd.

His urban New York scenes include Cliff Dwellers, showing a crowded Lower East Side tenement street with clotheslines and a trolley heading toward Vesey Street (with a woman breastfeeding in the summer heat). This painting used a color system based on musical notes learned from H.G. Maratta. 42 Kids depicts the title number of skinny-dipping children on a wooden pier known as 'Splinter Beach' on the East River.

Bellows also created a controversial series of WWI atrocity paintings, including The Barricade (German soldiers using naked Belgians as a human shield), The Germans Arrive (an officer holding a boy whose hands were severed), and a painting of the execution of nurse Edith Cavell. When Joseph Pennell and others criticized him for painting scenes he hadn't witnessed, Bellows famously retorted that Leonardo da Vinci didn't have 'a ticket to paint the Last Supper.'

Other notable works include seascapes inspired by Winslow Homer from his visits to Monhegan and Matinicus Islands in Maine (including a painting of gulls over Monhegan Island), The Big Dory (angular men moving a red-and-white boat), a portrait of the buck-toothed Paddy Flannigan, a lithograph of evangelist Billy Sunday, a series on the construction of Pennsylvania Station, a lithograph of a lynching victim made with collaborator Bolton Brown, and the painting Steaming Streets.
recursive_suggestions: Ashcan School — Bellows's primary movement; frequently cross-referenced
Robert Henri — Bellows's teacher, founder of The Eight
Thomas Eakins — boxing paintings (Salutat, Between Rounds) frequently paired with Bellows
